# Correlation rule — beaconing / C2 cadence (T1071)

**Suggested severity**: Low · **Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Scheduled correlation rule: a host making many connections (`conns > 60`) to a single destination with very few distinct ports (`ports < 3`) per hour — the steady, low-variety pattern of C2 beaconing. `bin _time span = 1h` + `comp count() / count_distinct(action_remote_port) by _time, agent_hostname, action_remote_ip`. Keep this at LOW severity: it is high-volume / lower-confidence, so it surfaces as issues WITHOUT opening a case per hit (Low issues aren't grouped into cases). Tune `conns`/`ports` to your environment; add suppression on (agent_hostname, action_remote_ip) to collapse repeats.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
| fields _time, agent_hostname, action_remote_ip, action_remote_port
| bin _time span = 1h
| comp count() as conns, count_distinct(action_remote_port) as ports by _time, agent_hostname, action_remote_ip
| filter conns > 60 and ports < 3
| fields _time, agent_hostname, action_remote_ip, conns, ports
```

_Correlation-rule XQL body live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (runs to SUCCESS with confirmed fields). The schedule, time window, threshold-tuning, severity, MITRE mapping and suppression are rule-wrapper settings in the XSIAM editor — not in the XQL. See the `cortex_detection_rule_authoring` skill._
